title: placus odoratus
intro: Placus odoratus, also known as the stinking chamomile, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Lamiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Placus odoratus include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe digestive problems and reduce fever. The herbal preparations of Placus odoratus include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
